question:
Consider the semidefinite program over a symmetric 3x3 matrix X:
Minimize <C, X> subject to <A_i, X> = b_i for i=1..3, trace(X) = 23.0, and X is positive semidefinite (X ⪰ 0).C = [[-1, 3.5, 0.5], [3.5, 2, -0.5], [0.5, -0.5, 4]]
A_1 = [[2, 0, 1], [0, -2, 0.5], [1, 0.5, 4]]
A_2 = [[4, -0.5, 2], [-0.5, 4, 1], [2, 1, 3]]
A_3 = [[-1, 0, 2], [0, 3, 0], [2, 0, -3]]
b_1 = 46.0, b_2 = 83.0, b_3 = -17.0

What is the minimum value of <C, X> (rounded to 3 decimals)?

Consider the semidefinite program over a symmetric 3x3 matrix X:
Minimize <C, X> subject to <A_i, X> = b_i for i=1..3, trace(X) = 26.0, and X is positive semidefinite (X ⪰ 0).C = [[-4, -3, 1.5], [-3, -1, -2], [1.5, -2, 3]]
A_1 = [[2, -1, 0], [-1, -3, 1], [0, 1, -3]]
A_2 = [[4, -1.5, -1], [-1.5, 0, -1], [-1, -1, 0]]
A_3 = [[-3, 2.5, -3.5], [2.5, 2, -2.5], [-3.5, -2.5, -3]]
b_1 = -14.0, b_2 = 48.0, b_3 = -78.0

What is the minimum value of <C, X> (rounded to 3 decimals)?
